summary:
Belimumab has been shown to be effective in inducing remission in lupus nephritis through both regulatory approvals and robust clinical trials such as BLISS-LN and BEAT LUPUS, which demonstrate significant improvements in patient outcomes including reduction in disease activity markers and relapse rates. Its use supports a clinically validated approach to treatment initiation in this condition. 

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{Yes}}$

The summary should end with "Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{Yes}}$".

Belimumab is efficacious in treating lupus nephritis, evidenced by formal FDA approval for induction therapy and strong support from randomized controlled trials like BLISS-LN and BEAT LUPUS, which report reductions in disease activity, anti-DNA antibody levels, and flare frequencies. Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{Yes}}$
Based only on what was said above, summarize Agent1’s reasoning about whether belimumab works for lupus nephritis:

Belimumab is effective for lupus nephritis because it has received official approval for induction therapy and demonstrated positive results in major clinical trials—BLISS-LN showed better outcomes than placebo while BEAT LUPUS found lower autoimmune marker levels and less frequent flares after rituximab failure—which confirms consistent benefits across different stages of care.  
Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{Yes}}$

Only one final sentence must contain “Final Answer” followed by boxed text.

– The BLISS-LN trial shows improvement over controls 
– The BEAT LUPUS trial notes reduced anti-dsDNA antibodies and fewer flares especially when used after rituximab fails
